evals.prompt: Catch me up. What was I working on, what's recent, and what's available to start?
evals.expected_output: A briefing under 40 lines with sections for Active Work, Ready to Start, Recent Activity, Uncommitted Changes, Quick Stats, and (if signals exist) Housekeeping. Read-only; no files written.

assertions.text: Output is structured with the named sections: Active Work, Ready to Start, Recent Activity, Quick Stats
Output is under 40 lines (count the rendered briefing only, ignoring code fences)
The Recent Activity section lists git commits from the fixture (the fixture seeds at least 3 commits)
If the fixture's outputs/ contains a file older than 7 days, the Housekeeping section flags it and suggests `consolidate staging`
If the fixture's .memory/ contains active content, the Housekeeping section suggests considering `working promote`
The workspace contains no new files written by the briefing run (briefing is read-only — the file count and contents should match the fixture pre-run)

evals.expected_output: A briefing that handles the missing issue tracker gracefully — either asks the user which tracker to query, or proceeds with git-only sections and notes the gap.

assertions.text: Output either asks the user to specify an issue tracker, OR explicitly notes that no issue tracker is configured / detected, OR omits the tracker-dependent sections cleanly without erroring
Output still includes Recent Activity from git (git is available in the fixture)
Output does not invent or hallucinate issues — no IDs, no titles, no fake counts in the Active Work / Ready to Start sections

evals.expected_output: A short briefing reflecting an essentially-empty project state. No fabricated activity. 'Project is clear' or equivalent.

assertions.text: Output explicitly notes that there is no active or ready work (e.g., 'Project is clear', 'No open issues', 'Nothing in progress')
Output does NOT include a Housekeeping section (no staleness signals exist in this fixture)
Briefing under 20 lines for this trivial case
Output does not invent commits, issues, or activity that doesn't exist in the fixture
